question_text: Which of the following is the correct definition of speed?
options: The rate at which an object changes direction
The distance travelled divided by the time taken
The amount of force acting on an object
The total distance an object can travel before stopping
correct_answer: The distance travelled divided by the time taken
explanation: Speed measures how fast something is moving by comparing the distance covered to the time it took. This is the fundamental definition used in physics.
source_name: KS3 Physics Curriculum - Speed and Velocity
